Trippp · The Master Plan

Palaces.
Then mountains.

June 24 – July 1. While the monsoon swallows the rest of the country, we'll be in the only two places in India having their best week of the year. This isn't a holiday. This is the trip people ask us about for years.

24 JUN — 01 JUL · 8 DAYS · LAKES → PALACES → HIMALAYAS
Night 3 onwards: this is the balcony · Dal Lake, Srinagar
The window, per IMD — what's happening June 24 to July 1
Uttarakhand
Monsoon arriving. IMD pattern: heavy → very heavy rainfall in exactly our week. Landslide belt activates.
Rishikesh
Ganga rafting season legally closes June 30, every year. Routes can shut days earlier after pre-monsoon rain.
Mumbai / Goa
Full monsoon. Beach water sports shut, party season dormant. Atmospheric, but muted.
Udaipur
Early monsoon = the lakes fill, the palaces glow, temperatures drop. Built for this season — literally has a Monsoon Palace.
Kashmir
The exception. One of its driest, greenest stretches — short showers, clear mountain views, 14+ hour days, cherry season.

SOURCES: IMD MONSOON BULLETINS · UTTARAKHAND TOURISM RAFTING NOTICE · KASHMIR SEASONAL CLIMATE DATA — FULL LIST IN FOOTER

The contenders

Same Udaipur. Very different second acts.

Season
Closed
Plan A

Udaipur + Rishikesh + Valley of Flowers

The most ambitious plan on paper — and the one the calendar quietly kills. Both headline experiences are out of season in opposite directions: one just ended, one hasn't started.

  • The rafting we're going for ends as we land. Uttarakhand Tourism closes all Ganga rafting on June 30 — and operators warn late-June routes can close days early when pre-monsoon rain hits. We'd arrive June 26-ish for a season on life support.
  • The flowers aren't there yet. Valley of Flowers peak bloom is mid-July to mid-August. Late June = green valley, early sprouts, no famous flower carpet. We'd do the hard part and miss the payoff.
  • It's a 38 km, 4–6 day trek. Not a park. Govindghat → 13 km up to Ghangaria → 4 km into the valley, same-day exit mandatory, then all the way back. It doesn't fit an 8-day trip that also includes Udaipur. At all.
  • And it's the danger week. IMD's forecast pattern puts heavy-to-very-heavy rain over Uttarakhand right in our window. This is the state where monsoon road travel goes genuinely wrong — slippery trails, landslides, blocked highways.

Net: we'd spend two days driving into a flooding hill state to watch a closed rafting season and an unbloomed valley.

Fine.
Just fine
Plan C

Goa + Mumbai + Udaipur

The safe plan. Nothing here is dangerous — it's just three versions of the same register: city, coast, city. Good food, real nightlife, zero mountains.

  • Goa in monsoon is half-Goa. Beautiful and green, but water sports are shut, beach shacks packed up, party calendar dormant. We'd get the off-season edition.
  • Mumbai delivers, but in the rain. Food and nightlife don't care about weather — fair. But July Mumbai means commuting between meals through serious downpours.
  • Udaipur still shines. The one in-season pick, same as every plan.
  • No range. Our guest flies from the US once. This itinerary shows her three urban Indias and zero Himalayas — the thing she literally said she was "super down" to see.

Net: a nice trip nobody talks about in five years.

Approved
· Go ·
Plan B

Udaipur + Kashmir

Here's the part nobody clocked: Kashmir is Plan A without the failure modes. Same promises — white water, alpine valleys, the Himalayas — except every one of them is actually in season during our exact week.

  • It's the driest beautiful place in India right now. While the monsoon swallows the rest of the country, the Kashmir valley gets short refreshing showers and clear mountain light. Srinagar 22–32°C, Pahalgam 18–24°C, Gulmarg 14–19°C.
  • The meadows are at peak green. Betaab Valley, Aru Valley, Gulmarg, Sonamarg — emerald, snow-rimmed, and reachable by car. No 38 km trek, no permit, no gamble on bloom dates.
  • White water that's actually open. The Lidder river at Pahalgam runs beginner-friendly rafting in June — fed by snowmelt, in season, while Rishikesh shuts its gates.
  • The architecture thread continues. Udaipur's palaces → Srinagar's Mughal gardens (Nishat, Shalimar) → houseboat woodwork on Dal Lake. One aesthetic story across two worlds.
  • It IS the Himalayas. Gondola to ~4,000 m at Gulmarg for the big-peak panorama — without Spiti's altitude sickness or 20-hour approach drives.

Net: palaces and lakes, then mountains and meadows. The two most beautiful in-season places in the country, back to back.

The receipts

Everything Plan A promised, Kashmir delivers — in season.

"I want white water rafting"
Plan ARishikesh closes June 30 — we'd catch the last gasp, or nothing.
KashmirLidder river, Pahalgam — snowmelt-fed, beginner-friendly, peak season in late June.
"I want a beautiful valley"
Plan AValley of Flowers blooms mid-July+. We'd trek 38 km for the preview.
KashmirBetaab + Aru valleys: peak-green meadows under snow peaks, a scenic drive away. Zero trek required.
"I'm super down to see the Himalayas" — the guest
Plan AUttarakhand Himalayas, in their most dangerous week of the year.
KashmirThe Himalayas in their best week of the year. Gulmarg gondola to ~4,000 m, snow at the top, meadows below.
"And the architecture"
Plan A / CUdaipur, then nothing.
KashmirUdaipur's palaces, then the Mughal gardens of Srinagar — same imperial craft, alpine edition.
The plan, day by day

Eight days, two worlds.

Day 1
Jun 24

Bangalore → Udaipur

Direct flight, ~2h15. Afternoon at the City Palace — the largest in Rajasthan — then a sunset boat across Lake Pichola past Jag Mandir.

Day 2
Jun 25

Udaipur, properly

Jagdish Temple and the old-city lanes in the morning, Bagore ki Haveli on the lake, then sunset at Sajjangarh — the Monsoon Palace, doing the exact job it was built for.

Day 3
Jun 26

Udaipur → Srinagar

Fly via Delhi (~5–7h door to door — the one transit tax of the trip). Land into a different planet: evening shikara ride on Dal Lake, sleep on a carved-cedar houseboat.

BOOK AS ONE TICKET VIA DELHI · SXR AIRPORT: ARRIVE 2.5 HRS EARLY
Day 4
Jun 27

Pahalgam — the valley day

Drive the Lidder valley (~2.5h): Betaab Valley, Aru meadows, and white-water rafting on the Lidder. This is the "valley of flowers" experience, minus the trek, plus the rafting.

DO PAHALGAM EARLY — AMARNATH YATRA TRAFFIC BUILDS FROM ~JUL 3
Day 5
Jun 28

Gulmarg — the Himalaya day

One of the world's highest gondolas: Phase 1 to flower meadows, Phase 2 toward Apharwat (~4,000 m) for snow and big-peak views. Fleece weather in late June.

Day 6
Jun 29

Sonamarg or Doodhpathri

Pick one: Thajiwas glacier walk at Sonamarg, or the quieter milk-white meadows of Doodhpathri. Evening back in old Srinagar — bakeries, crafts, kahwa.

Day 7
Jun 30

Mughal gardens + the slow day

Nishat and Shalimar Bagh terraced over Dal Lake — the architecture finale. Cherry stalls on the Sopore road (June is peak cherry season), floating market if we're up at dawn.

Day 8
Jul 1

Srinagar → home

Fly out via Delhi. Or, if the group keeps Goa in play — Srinagar → Goa connections exist (~5–7h) and monsoon Goa makes a mellow landing pad before Bangalore.

Where we sleep

A palace on a lake. Then a boat on a lake.

Live prices for our exact dates, pulled today. Per room for two — split it and laugh.

Srinagar · The signature night

Sukoon Houseboat, Dal Lake

★ 4.8 · 168 reviews · from $419 / 2 nights · sun deck · free shikara transfers

The houseboat on Dal Lake — carved cedar suites, lotus gardens out the window, kahwa and live Sufi music on the deck, snow peaks behind. Reviewers call it "an experience of a lifetime," and they're not exaggerating. Two nights here, then a hotel for the rest.

Udaipur · The flex

Taj Lake Palace

★ 4.9 · 6,624 reviews · $588 / 2 nights

A marble palace floating in the middle of Lake Pichola. You arrive by boat. Monsoon pricing makes the impossible briefly possible.

Udaipur · The smart luxury play

Taj Fateh Prakash Palace

★ 4.8 · 3,372 reviews · $285 / 2 nights

Inside the City Palace complex itself, staring straight at the Lake Palace. Half the price, arguably the better view.

Udaipur · The value pick

Hotel Lakend

★ 4.8 · 4,965 reviews · $160 / 2 nights

On Fateh Sagar lake with gardens to the water. Best rating-to-price ratio in the city.

Srinagar · Nights 5–7

Hotel The Dewan by Royal Naqash

★ 4.8 · 291 reviews · ~$46 / night

The post-houseboat base: top-rated, easy on the budget, leaves room in the wallet for pashmina and saffron.

LIVE PRICING: TRIPADVISOR · CHECKED TODAY FOR 24 JUN – 01 JUL · ILLUSTRATIONS ARE OURS

Eat like you mean it

Two cuisines, zero overlap.

Udaipur · Mewari

Royal Rajasthan on a plate

Order: dal baati churma (the state dish — baked wheat balls, dal, sweet crumble), laal maas (fiery red mutton curry), gatte ki sabzi, and ker sangri if you spot it — a desert-bean dish you won't find anywhere else.

The move: one rooftop dinner facing the lit-up City Palace across Lake Pichola. Dinner with that view is half the reason people fall for Udaipur.

dal baati churmalaal maasgatte ki sabzirooftop + palace view
Kashmir · Wazwan

The feast cuisine of India

Order: rogan josh (the original, nothing like restaurant versions elsewhere), gushtaba (silky mutton meatballs in yogurt), yakhni, dum aloo, and nadru (lotus stem) for the vegetarian win.

Don't skip: kahwa — saffron-almond green tea, everywhere, perfect after cold meadow days. Fresh kandur bakery bread in old Srinagar at dawn. And it's cherry season — roadside stalls on the Sopore road sell the best cherries in Asia for pocket change.

rogan joshgushtabakahwakandur breadJune cherries
The shot list

Where the camera earns its keep.

Udaipur

Golden hour city

Gangaur Ghat / Ambrai Ghat at sunrise — palace reflections, no crowds. City Palace balconies framing Lake Pichola. The boat to Jag Mandir — shoot back toward the palace from the water. Sajjangarh (Monsoon Palace) at sunset, above the clouds if the rain gods cooperate. Monsoon light here is moody and saturated — better than peak-season haze.

Kashmir

The reels write themselves

Shikara on Dal Lake at golden hour — the signature shot. Houseboat interiors — carved cedar everywhere. Betaab Valley bridge over the Lidder. Gulmarg gondola rising over green meadows, then snow at Apharwat in late June — snow selfies while Bangalore melts. Nishat Bagh terraces stepping down to the lake, and Pari Mahal above the city at dusk. Dawn floating vegetable market on Dal if anyone's up at 5.

Pack

One bag, two climates.

For Udaipur · 30°C+, humid, bursts of rain

Light layer

Breathable cottons/linens, sunglasses, sunscreen, a compact umbrella or packable rain shell. Sandals fine in the city. Shoulders/knees covered for temple visits.

For Kashmir · 14–32°C depending on altitude

Warm layer

A fleece + light jacket — Gulmarg evenings hit single digits, and Apharwat at ~4,000 m is genuinely cold even in June. Grippy walking shoes for wet meadow trails. Sunscreen is non-negotiable: UV at altitude burns fast. Motion-sickness tabs for the winding hill drives if anyone's prone.

Everywhere

The small stuff that saves the trip

Power bank (in cabin baggage only at Srinagar airport — never checked), basic meds, copies of ID (checkpoints in Kashmir may ask), and a duffel with spare room — see souvenirs below, you'll need it.

Bring home

The souvenir run.

Udaipur

Miniatures and leather

Mewar miniature paintings (watch artists work in old-city studios), leather-bound journals, silver jewelry, bandhani textiles. Haggle gently — start around 60% of quoted.

Kashmir

The serious haul

Pashmina — the real thing, but the market is flooded with viscose fakes. Buy from government-approved emporiums or shops that show the GI tag; real pashmina passes through a ring and won't spark under a lighter test. Beyond that: saffron (Pampore-grown, world's best), walnut-wood carving, papier-mâché boxes, kahwa blends, dried apricots and walnuts, and hand-knotted carpets if anyone's feeling rich.

Know before you go

The stuff nobody tells you.

SIM cards · the big one

Prepaid SIMs from the rest of India don't work in J&K

Land in Srinagar with a Bangalore prepaid SIM and your phone is offline. Carry a postpaid SIM, or buy a local J&K SIM at Srinagar airport on arrival. Sort this before flying — especially for our US guest, whose roaming may also behave differently there.

Cash

UPI gets unreliable outside Srinagar

Card machines and UPI are patchy in Pahalgam, Gulmarg and Sonamarg — dhabas, pony stands and small hotels run on cash, and ATMs run dry on busy days. Withdraw in Srinagar; carry more than feels necessary.

The vibe check

Kashmir is largely dry

Alcohol availability is very limited — a handful of licensed hotel bars in Srinagar, and that's about it. If the group wants drinks, Udaipur is the leg for it. Kashmir's nightlife is kahwa on a houseboat deck, and honestly that's the point.

Logistics, locked

Book early, fly smart

Amarnath Yatra season means Srinagar hotels fill 6–8 weeks out — book now, not later. Srinagar airport: arrive 2.5 hrs early, multiple security checks, batteries in cabin bags. Book Udaipur→Srinagar as a single through-ticket via Delhi so a delayed first leg doesn't strand us. Dress modestly at temples, mosques and shrines on both legs.

This is what 7 AM looks like · Sukoon sundeck, Dal Lake
The verdict

One week. Two kingdoms.

A marble palace floating on a lake. A cedar boat under snow peaks. Valleys so green they look rendered, rafting that's actually open, and the Himalayas our guest crossed an ocean for. All of it in season. All of it in eight days.

Vote Udaipur + Kashmir. Then start packing.
Receipts
· IMD monsoon bulletins, June 2026 — onset over Kerala Jun 4; northwest-India advance late Jun–early Jul; heavy-to-very-heavy rainfall pattern over Uttarakhand in the Jun 22–26 band
· Uttarakhand Tourism / Ganga Nadi Rafting Rotation Samiti — annual rafting closure Jun 30; early route closures possible after pre-monsoon rain
· Valley of Flowers NP — opens Jun 1; peak bloom mid-Jul to mid-Aug; ~38 km round trip over 4–6 days; day-entry only via Ghangaria
· Kashmir seasonal data — Jun among driest months; Srinagar 22–32°C / Pahalgam 18–24°C / Gulmarg 14–19°C; gondola operational; Lidder rafting in season; Amarnath Yatra from early Jul
· Flight data — BLR–UDR direct ~2h15; UDR–SXR via Delhi ~5–7h; SXR–GOI 1-stop ~5–7h
